title: padus serotina f. pendula
intro: Padus serotina f. pendula, also known as the American dogwood, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America. The plant's bark and berries have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, possessing antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Padus serotina f. pendula are believed to contribute to its health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, can be made from its bark, leaves, and berries, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
